text: Customer Service is useless and incompetent! Shame on us for supporting this terrible quality of service.

I’ve been with T-mobile since 2004, 17 years now. Their connection has never been great but I’ve stayed because of their once good customer service. Well, that has changed now. As soon as I return to the states from this trip, I will switch to a different company. 

If you’re traveling abroad and receive a text message from them saying you can buy a $5/day international card, DON’T! They really don’t mean a day; you’re really just buying the amount of data you can use and it can be up within 15 minutes of usage like what had happened to me. This is the exact text message I received before sending them $5.

“Welcome to Greece! Your T-Mobile plan gives you unlimited data at up to 2G speeds and texts at no extra cost. Calls at 25 cents/min. Want our fastest data and unlimited calls? Get an International Pass for just $5/day. Check it out: .... Enjoy your stay!“

It clearly says $5/day and when I called to dispute about it, the customer service rep acknowledged that it was misleading, yet still tried to sell me a 10 day pass with 5GB for more money. I told her, again, it’s not a 10 day pass, is it? What you’re saying is do I want to pay $$$ for 5GB of data? She said yes, that’s what it means even though that’s not how it’s presented. Wow! There was more to the conversation and she even indicated that I was lying about their poor connection service because other people calling in from the city I’m from all report that they have good connection. I went ok, so people call in just to tell you that they have good connection? Then she asked me to give them a call when I was at the area with poor connection so that they could make improvements for that area. I said how can I call if there is poor connection or no service period?! My god! I told her I would leave a bad review about this experience so that others wouldn’t lose their money for such a scam. And here it is. Buyer beware at its best!

Wow , I have been a customer for 15 years and service, customer service dropped right off the radar once they merged. I waited on hold over an hour for a technician support. I got a customer service instead and she told me to change my phone number if I am having any problems and then put me on hold again . After another hour on hold I gave up . The recording keeps repeating “ at T-Mobile you get to talk to a real person “. 
What a joke . Recording also says “no bouncing around from rep to rep “. Don’t suggest t mobile anymore. Had a hard time understanding the rep as well . I don’t recommend this company. Name change should be … no service TMobile !!

Changed from Verizon a month ago. 1. The got my email wrong for Netflix, had to go back to the store to fix it. 2. Text messages stopped working, two more trips to the store then told to call 611. That took half an hour for a callback, the call dropped twice. ANother 611 call about the text messages. Two returned calls the automated attendant asks to enter 1 if ready to talk. Twice the service would not take the 1 and eventually disconnected. I got a message that my request for a refund for the balance on my Verizon phone was rejected and that someone would call. No one ever called. I tried to chat and got a response, but after explaining what was happening chat stopped replying. Drove the Verizon store and converted back.

I've been a T-Mobile customer for almost 14 years. My plan keeps getting modified, charging more and giving less (like losing international calling which I now have to pay for on one line as an add-on, taking off my discount because "it was never supposed to be allowed for the plan I was on". 
I had 4 lines for all those years, recently added a 5th with a watch, and was on the original jump on demand program where I just lease the phone and can upgrade every few months, but I usually just upgrade around the year mark. They don't offer that program anymore but said I'm "grandfathered" in. They've made so many changes to it that it is a hassle to get a new phone, and now they charge an upfront price difference, higher monthly payments, etc. I'm sure they are trying to get people to switch off of it. I just dealt with it and stayed on the program because it made more sense than buying the phone, although the savings were minimal, a few dollars per month. 
Even with those changes I still liked their service for the most part, although there were many times friends and family would have service with Verizon and my T-Mobile wouldn't. The difference in price and good reliability through T-Mobile kept me a customer. 
Ever since they started merging with Sprint my service has went to crap. Half of a conversation cuts out where either I can't hear the other caller or they can't hear me. Some texts not going through or received, slow service, feels like I am back using my very first smart phone from 14 years ago. 
It has caused a lot of issues, especially since I use one line to run my business. I have gotten 2 new phones for my work line since May both S21's through the warranty, but speaking with others who have T-Mobile, I see now it is not my phone but the service, they all notice a difference too.
During the last phone call with a customer service rep, he said that they are upgrading a lot of towers in my area including the ones my phone uses regularly. Why they would upgrade all of them at the exact same time... The rep said they usually take a month or maybe 2 for the full upgrade, although mentioned the ones here have been getting worked on since May and June and its almost November. 
I recently switched my phone off the 5g network and it did improve the issues, now I only miss about 20% of the conversation. 
Way back when I was a Nextel customer and they merged with Sprint, that coverage went to crap too, and that's when made the switch to T-Mobile. Ironic now that Sprint is merging with T-Mobile the same thing is happening.
The trust and reliability I had with T-Mobile is gone. I am currently looking into other carriers before T-Mobile ends up causing me to lose clients because of these issues. It almost happened this week but I was able to save it. Enough is enough, and now that its gotten to the point of affecting my business and livelihood, its time to move on. Will be switching in the next day or two.

I needed to adjust our reservation by 1 night due to a family emergency and called to do that this morning. It was the last day to change anything.  I spoke to a horribly rude young lady named April from T-Mobile Travel and she was absolutely awful. She didn't know how to adjust the dates, so I kindly asked to speak with a manager to see if they knew how to adjust the dates. She told me he was too busy and had other calls and advised me to just cancel the reservation I made 4 months ago & re-do it. I asked her if he could call me and she argued and argued that she could help me after she just told me she could not help me. She finally said he would call me and asked when he should call. I said anytime and she said that it had to be a specific time when she entered it, so I chose a time and not surprisingly, I did not receive a return call. She told me that they MIGHT be able to call the hotel AFTER today, once it's too late to cancel my reservation, and see if the hotel would allow an adjustment in our days. After getting off the phone I called our hotel and spoke with the manager and he said that shouldn't be a problem and they would be happy to adjust our days but that it had to be done through T-Mobile Travel since I paid through them. I just received an email from T-Mobile Travel that said the hotel would not allow an adjustment. I have had the WORST experience with T-Mobile Travel and I will be sure to pass that on.

We have been with Tmobile for 13+ years and have never had problems with them until they merged with Sprint.  There were a couple of problems.  First, we found out that we were actually paying to lease our phones from Sprint.  Second, when a Sprint Associate called my husband to say that we could get new free phones if we turned in our Tmobile phones and we did. No, they were not free. That happened a little after they combined.  And just recently, about 2 months ago, we had to go through so much hassle just to put our phones and service back to Tmobile, because we've always liked their service and coverage, we stayed with T-mobile.  However, when we get our first bill after transferring back it was almost doubled the amount we usually pay! The Tmobile Associate at the store that helped me had put all our phones on insurance when I didn't ask for it and she put us on the highest priced plan!! And for 5 phones, that was too much. I had to call Customer Service, which took forever, and got it changed to the Essentials plan, which is the lowest priced plan.  We just want service to use our phones.  We don't need all the extras.  It's unnecessary.  And now, to top it off, Sprint is saying we owe them $200 for service for when the phones were transferring back to tmobile!!!  Talking to Customer Service on both ends is like talking to a wall.  Why combine if you can't service as one company??!!  We're definitely changing services.

There is a reason why T Mobile has an 84% "Bad" rating. I have been on the phone for over an hour trying to reactivate my T Mobile Hotspot. I paid $90.00 for the device, was told I could activate and reactivate as I like (at $50.00 for 30 days) since I only need it occasionally while traveling. Turns out if you don't reactivate within 45 days from last deactivation, your expensive hotspot turns into a brick. Throw it away. This is something they conveniently don't tell you when purchasing the device. So basically, you need to keep the device active (and pay $50.00 per month) whether you need it or not. I don't recommend anyone purchase a T Mobile hotspot.

I’m so disgusted with T-Mobile right now this is not how you treat a custom that been with you guys for 15 years i this point I wouldn’t recommend no one to T-Mobile you guys don’t stand by your word you treat new customers better than someone who been with you for years so tell me y I should stay with this company I’m waiting to hear from you 862-287-3246 eric williams

edits.text: Im a loyal customer that been with you guys for over 15 years I’m having a problem with my phone and can’t get any help but you guys are running promotions and giving away phones to new customers I can’t seem to grasp that shouldn’t you be taking care of your loyal customers before they think about changing there service my bill is always paid no problems up and til now this is sad for someone that’s been with you for 15 years I will be looking forward to hearing from someone 862-287-3246 Eric unsatisfied customer

text: Worst telecommunication company in United States.

T-mobile is horrible. Do NOT go to them. If you pay for a phone straight out, they will not unlock them. Our daughter bought us both an iPhone 12 max pro. Paid straight out for them. Our phone bill jumped to $400. When calling to find out why they were no help. My wife and I each had a phone, why would a bill for two people be $400. So we canceled it. I got a text that 8 lines are no longer attached to our account. 8 lines, we are 2 people with a phone each, that our daughter paid in full. Why would there be 8 lines and why couldn’t they tell me this on the phone. We canceled service and wanted to unlock our phones to use with another company. We called several time and was told each time that they cannot be unlocked. Our daughter spent a lot on these phones and they are worthless. T-mobile takes advantage of the elderly thinking we wouldn’t figure it out. Horrible company!

T-Mobile Has Terrible Service and No resolutions!
I went with them as a new customer in May 21 with the terrible One Plus phone.  Nothing but trouble, I spend hrs at time calling them.  My phone has NO INTERNET OR MOBILE DATA, THEY MESSAGED ME MY LINE AS A BLOCK ON IMPORTANT MESSAGES, THEY DID NOT FIX!  The give me partial credits here and there.  What am I paying for?  Making calls only?  No map directions either.  Delays with missed calls to next day!  Tonight was it!  Reported them on the rip off report, etc.  Of course Im mad and it is causing trouble with work because I use it for work purposes too!  The so called supervisor tonight told me because Im upset shet can't help me AND even said I quote Tmobile isn't for you, unquote.  You bet and now every time I call my call is routed to her only.  She said she is the only one I will be able to talk to like she is God or the Pope!  Ok, I told her she is right Tmobile is not for me!  Now we will be doing this on my terms as I have had it.  I want out of this contract with no monies owed and port my phone number I can over with and transfer my info off phone before factory reset and I will go elsewhere.  Gues what, crickets!! No response!  I then told her I will file a complaint everywhere and when we are done I feel they owe me for the trouble this is causing in my life!
They are a rip off and I also told her that her comment of tmobile is not for me, means they live up to their terrible reputation!.  This is borderline fraud!  Their mono is when you are fed up and angry, nother words when the kitchen gets hot, they are gone!!

I been with Tmobile for 15 years than transfer to Verizon and back to Tmobile for the last two years. Their customer service was amazing until this last few months. Wait time is never less then 45 mins even when they tell you 30 mins call back. Tried to transfer accounts from my sister in law. Everything was done and to right now I been on the phone with them for the past 5 days 8 hours on the phone. 3 trips to the store for nothing to have happen. No one calls you back, they just transfer you around until you give up. If I wasn't military in the plan I would just leave. Worse cell phone customer service I have ever had.

I have never been lied to more iny life 
10 years
Needed to return new a52 7 calls later I still have it. Done.

Terrible service in store and on the phone. Sprint offers are not acknowledge on the t-mobile stores, they just want to upgrade t-mobile customers to higher fee plans. The sprint app give free phone upgrades that they don't acknowledge if you try to trade a different phone of higher credits if the phone is not your actual. On the phone, they have no clue at all and just keep talking about how wonderful life will be with t-mobile new plans. I am already on t-mobile network and i just want the deal that shows in my sprint app as free upgrade. Time to move to another carrier. They are making it really difficult

T-Mobile's Customer "service" is terrible! When you call them the average waiting time is 45 minutes! This is totally unacceptable! They REALLY don't care about their customers. Since they bought SPRINT it became even much worst than before!

I see a lot of negative reviews for T-Mobile but my personal experience has been wonderful. I just ordered a iPhone 13 Pro Max & the customer service was amazing!! A wonderful gentleman named Aziz is a saint!! I pick up my phone this week & he has offered to help me set it up, transfer data from my old phone, & help me add a new line for my Daughter. As an additional  bonus he has agreed to help me apply the screen protector (I have trouble getting it on right). If that isn’t outstanding customer service then I don’t know what is. I can’t speak for other stores BUT our T-Mobile goes above & beyond!! Thank you Aziz!!!  Love these guys!!

I am not one to vent online, but this issue has pushed me over the limit. T-Mobile is so unorganized, unprofessional, misleading that I have to let everyone know NOT to choose them. I have been into a t-mobile store two days straight and one of my days spending over 2 hours for them to NOT resolve my issues! I had sprint and transferred my account information over to t-mobile and no one can give me straight forward answer about how things work. I have called customer service and they have transferred me from one rep to another and I still have not got my issue resolved! I then went back to the T-mobile store and the store rep is not telling me the same thing that customer service is saying. I am just so confused how T-mobile purchased sprint and they are silently working as two different entities because sprint can not see or handle t-mobile issues and t-mobile can not see or handle sprint issues. This company is one big joke and I advise EVERYONE to stay clear of them! I was on a call with customer service for one hour and fourth five mins and the representative hung up on me because I asked to speak to a supervisor and I NEVER got a call back, even tho at the beginning of the call I was asked if my number was the call back number just in case we got disconnected. bye bye T-mobile.. u just lost a valued customer of 5+ years!

I decided to change services and went withT-Mobile. I ported in a current number and added a new line, so I got 2 lines of service. During the signup process, I was promised a $250 gift card. They said that I would get it in 4-6 weeks. It's now been 6 months and I still haven't gotten it.

When I reached out to T-Mobile, they said that I wouldn't be getting a gift card, because they ran out? Yeah, not cool. I'll be cancelling my service today and moving to a new carrier.

DO NOT USE T-MOBILE! THEY DO NOT FOLLOW THROUGH WITH THEIR PROMISES.
IF YOU GO A ROAD TRIP YOU LOSE THE SIGNAL IN THE RULAR AREAS THERE IS NO SIGNAL IF YOU LOSE A CONTACT YOU CANNOT MAKE THE CALL HISTORY IN THE WEBSITE ACCOUNT.

I've spent over a year now with specialists who promised me good service when their towers were upgraded.. well they are now upgraded and my service is worse than ever with magenta max!! I get bs service and lies to appease me fix it or we go to court over a year of this I have all the proof I need!! Im done with rep lies!!! Period!!!
Run, do not walk away from this cell service provider.  Customer service is ZERO.
Don’t use them

I actually liked T-mobile for the most part but their customer service is Terrible!  I got a job on the Navajo reservation where they don't have service.  I'm teaching high school and traveling 600 miles on the weekend to see my mom.  I've been getting the "you're at your max with roaming" messages but today I just got a text that they are going to cancel my service. Not a call or anything. Don't worry T-mobile I am done with you forever!!

I dropped my phone and the screen shattered also leaving my screen unresponsive to the touch. I took a visit to T-mobile as usual in hopes of finding Jailene there. Jailene has always had exceptional service skills while working fast and efficiently. I was out of t mobile with my upgraded iPhone 13 and some accessories within the hour. Hands down always the best place to come and if you’re looking for someone to guide you in your cell phone services- ask Jailene.

extremely bad experience. One agent after the other promises something which they cannot honor or deliver!  their systems are outdated. Agent indicated they cannot send an email in writing to confirm what they are stating. The next time I call to follow through on what was promised I am told the previous agent made a mistake.. this happened thrice unbelievable .. and this is a national level telecom player.. stay away from them for sure...

Terrible company. We set up my elderly father in law with a basic $20/month for a senior phone call and text plan. He called them because his phone wasn’t working quite right, and …somehow… he ended up with a $50/month plan of unlimited international texts, 22 gigs of high speed data and a protection plan on an iPhone 5 (when the 12 was just released)! Exploitation of a vulnerable adult!

T-Mobile customer service is the most unhelpful full customer service line I've ever had to deal with they don't call you back if you're disconnected They'll have you waiting for over 30 minutes The representatives are rude and unknowledgeable Furthermore the service that T-Mobile provides It's not good I'm always losing signal And dropping calls And they and they never have a way to fix it

I have been with T-Mobile for a number of years now recently they just automatically change my plan without my authorization charging me an additional $20 per month I called about five times every time the person promised that they will refund my money and it’s been going on for about six months now apparently all they keep giving us false promises

Service was mediocre for the first 6 months (town of 90K, living downtown) and has been declining since.  Data is effing useless any time humans are awake.  You want to game?  Sure, 2am to 5am COD will load.  Otherwise you better have stable wifi.  OnePlus fone was a big mistake as well.  I'm just another dummy who fell for a 24 month commitment on a piece of junk.  Doing my second replacement in 2 months, 3rd total.  $20 a pop.  Can't recommend this cr*p service to anyone.  Hell, straight talk was better.

edits.text: T-mobile is an upgrade from the pre-paids like boost and straight-talk (I've used both).  Phone coverage is much improved.  But the data rates are lacking, even off-peak.  If you want to game you will need to do it when most people are at work or asleep in your area, otherwise you are getting powned by the zitty kid with the strong wifi connect.

text: T-Mobile gave me the understanding that Stateside International Calling would be added to my bill. It was not. I received a $411 bill. They would not adjust it.

T-Mobile advertised a phone trade-in on Father's Day weekend. The trade-in was valued at $900.00 so I traded in my Galaxy S9 for a S21 at a T-Mobile store in Pasadena, California. Everything was fine. I paid the extra taxes and fees and went home with my new phone. Months later I noticed I was being overcharged so I called T-Mobile and they confirmed it was their mistake and assured me they would fix the problem. My account showed I owed the full $900.00 for the trade-in. They reversed the charges, but two months later it happened again, so I called and was again assured the problem was fixed. But two months later I was overcharged even more. T-Mobile then stated that I owe for the trade-in phone and say my honest transaction never happened. I am being charged for this trade-in. I was scammed by them and feel robbed/violated.

Crystie she was a good person to help me out with my bill I love her for what she did for my family  thank you god bless her and her family and her co workers family to t mobile the best phone company

I have been with T-Mobile since it was called voice stream every since they took on other carriers the wait time is too long on the phone in the store they don't want to cancel my items they want me to get it and then take it back to UPS the worst customer service and when you call and ask to be called back they call you back and still put you on hold I have never experienced customer service like this mind you I was with them when they were called voice stream

T-mobile's initial customer service was awesome.  Unfortunately their coverage was unreliable in key places - including my home and my office.   I finally gave up on it and decided to cancel - thinking that I might come back when they've built out their network further.  That's when the nightmare began.  Canceling was horrendous.  I have had to go through the process of canceling two times now -- spending most of the day trying to cancel each time.  When I was finally able to cancel the first time, I still ended up paying more than what I thought I had agreed to, upon cancelation.  Then I started seeing additional bills going beyond the extended payment period - and mentioning a phone number that I had not seen before.  I've been trying to cancel all day.  Calling in multiple times...waiting hours on end...and finally getting someone on the phone long enough to have a conversation - but hung up on me after I challenged him on why I need to provide a pin when I canceled all of my services months ago & I am not even sure if I can find the pin anymore.  They have made it so painful to cancel or to even challenge an erroneous charge for a phantom service - that it is criminal level.
Waited for an hour in the Tomball TX store only for the store rep to tell me I would need to call 611.

T mobile store sets up appointments then doesn't honor them. There is very inefficient management of time. The employees waste a lot of time and it seems to buy a phone is just as .uch time as buying a car. Will not ever return.

These people will lie and cheat you and never pay with cash. They are dishonest
And completely incompetent. The retail store will axe you to call their customer service
Line, whom will tell you to go back to the retail store.

Moreover, if you leave an urban area and any four lane highway, you will most likely
Lose service. Their service map is complete bull, as many of the rural area has no service.
And if they do, once you leave a small town, within two miles your cell phone will no longer
Be of use, other than to call 911. I have traveled as much as 30 miles without any service;
This does not happen with Verizon. This is why folks in rural country do not use Terrible Mo Bile.

This also applies to the off-brand cell providers using T-Mo Bile cell system.

All one star reviewers are welcome to party with me, once this worthless company goes bankrupt.

I just spent all day from 6am to 5:30 pm trying to get T-mobile to switch the shipping address for my sim card. The person who wrote up the order had it shipped via ups to the address of the local post office. Not even to my POB, just to the PO- which UPS can't deliver to. I was informed that though this was their mistake, it was my job to get it fixed. They repeatedly claimed that only ups could change the address on the order. UPS repeatedly said that tmobile had written the order so that the address could not be changed without their permission. I spoke to at least nine different T-mobilers, to whom I had to explain over and over what the problem was. They were all confused about whether my order even existed- though I had a confirmation number, a PIN number for the account, and multiple automated email confirmations from them. Though it is normal for some people to keep a phone and phone number from a previous account, none of the workers seemed to understand this. 
When after many hours of this hell i asked someone for a contact # for complaints, he gave me a fake link. He then told me he had made an appointment for me with my local tmobile store- which informed me they never make appointments. Finally, the guy at the local store spent 90 minutes going through the same hell, could not get anyone at hq to make any sense at all. He gave up and suggested I go to the PO and beg them to do something. I quite lost interest, after spending nearly 12 hours just trying to get the sim card sent to the right place. 
The following Monday, I then tried to communicate to them my intention to cancel my service. This entailed another 5 hours over two days. Sample dialogue: ME: the phone # for the account is XXX- it's a Verizon number, because I was going to keep my phone and number with T-mobile THEM: that's a Verizon number, that can't be right ME: yes, it's a Verizon number, I was going to keep it... THEM: I just tried to call you on it, it's not working because it was a temporary T-mobile number. ME: no, it's a Verizon number and it is still working. I never had a T-mobile number. THEM: I'd love to help you, but I need the phone number on your account. Can you remember your t-mobile number?..... on and on and on. To make it worse, each and every person online starts out with a lengthy, wordy, rambling, nearly obsequious speech about their deep concern for the customer- and then repeatedly refuses to deal with the problem, tells you they don't believe you know your own phone number and maybe your account wasn't even with t-mobile, etc...
Where does t-mobile find these people? --- I finally got it across to someone, I THINK, that I was canceling and wished a refund. I am not counting on it, but consider $28 a small price to pay to escape from these people. 
Turns out Tracfone has the exact same super-cheap plan, which took me all of two minutes to sign up for online.

T-Mobile offers the worst cell service in history.  Pathetic company that is piling on new customers while letting their existing customers rot in cell phone purgatory.  I am in the process of switching service asap.

Called tmobile wanting to change carrier, I was informed that I could get the Magenta Max plan and receive the Sansung S 20 Fe on them. Went through the application process, and was approved but was told I would have to put $144 down for the phone! Watch out! I have excellent credit they want to rip you off! You only have to put money down if you are not credit worthy. TMobile is a scam!

edits.text: I spent over 2 hours online with tmobile trying to switch services from Verizon, with a telesales associate in Missouri and was assured my phone, Galaxy A50 I have will work with the switch and keep promo they are running. I filled out all required paperwork, including having my social security number used to pull my credit.The sales associate dropped off and when I got back in touch with Tmobile, they then claimed my phone will not work! Watch out! Tmobile works a cool process to get your information  to sell to others for profit! I have a needless credit pull on my bureau as a result!

text: Moved to the US a year ago and my brother add me to his plan. My mom changed from another company to T-Mobile and we ordered a SIM card and sign up for a plan. My brother said he could add her to his plan and we called the same day to cancel before SIM card was even mailed out. They mailed it out regardless and advise me that the plan was cancelled. I called several times about it. Ask to speak to supervisor and they hang up on me. This all happened in August. They took $30 for administration fee which I was never informed about. I called they refund it. Come September they too $50 dollars and sent me a bill. I called over 5 times to sort it out and everyone keeps apologizing and every agent I spoke to gave me different excuses and said it will be credited to my account. We are in October my last call was 3 days ago I was told by a Supervisor it would have been credited by today and he would call to make sure it was credited. As of this day still nothing. I am not calling again and I will be changing to another company next month. No way they should still be profiting off me.

I have had T-Mobile service for over three years during that time I have purchased and paid off the following: 3 iPhone X, pad pro 12.9 tablet, and 1 iPhone 11 Pro Max.  I am trying to upgrade with the promotion they are offering, but they are requesting a $1000. Down payment.  I have paid my bill each and every month and they won’t budge on the down payment.  Stay away from this kind of apathetic attitude. Will take my business elsewhere.

If I could give 0 stars I would. T-mobile is the worst provider I have ever encountered. They scam people! I signed a purchase contract for 3 iphone 11’s and somehow ended up in a lease instead! Then when it came time to buy out my lease they could only place 2 phones on the payment plan because their agent suggested i change the number of one of my phones that was stolen and did not tell me it would affect the payment plan options of the lease. The manager promised a 60$ credit for my inconvenience of having to go into the store to sign a payment arrangement for the 3 rd phone, but not only do they not offer that service at the store but the manager never applied the credit to my account. The manager also promised 100$ credit for 5 mths to be applied to my account and there also was no record of that in the reps notes, conveniently! I have had nothing but problems and more headaches being with T-Mobile than their service is worth and would NOT recommend T-Mobile to anyone! Steer clear of this cell phone company, they will scam you, make false promises, and then they wont fix the issues even if their reps mess up! They do not care that our time is valuable too, and that we work hard for our money to pay our bills! I cannot wait to pay my phones off and leave T-Mobile without looking back! Warning: Don’t trust this company period!!!

I would rather have given NO stars! After being with tmobile for 20 years I will be leaving. After waiting over 1 hour at the Highland, Indiana store, the guy talked to me for a few minutes, said he would be right back, came back, put a tablet on the table, told another woman that he would help her now, looked at me a minute or so later and said he would be with me shortly. Really? What kind of service is that? I'm very disappointed in tmobile. Will be going somewhere else.

Just traded in my iPhone 11 Pro. As TMobile has advertised up to $800 credit for turning in a phone. Come to find out they will not give you much money. I should have just kept the phone and sold it 3rd Party. They only gave me $254 for an 11 pro! They are cheating people out of money. Don’t do the trade in!

Back in October I initially wrote a review that the T-Mobile location on Call Blvd, Wichita Falls was great and assisted with the transfer of my service. We went to the store as we had terrible issues with the phone "Customer Service". Should have seen that as a sign...Two months later, my "5" stars is a ZERO!
In November they removed the promo from my phone as they stated it was damaged when it was received. Interesting enough, the T-Mobile store handled my phone to transfer my data and packaged it up so all I had to do was mail it. So not sure where and how the "damage" happened.
I gave them and the phone Customer Service Department the opportunity to make it right. I made calls and went back to the store. Jess "Manager" told me she would work on it and get me the $800 promo.  That was on "Black Friday. She asked to give her a week. I gave 9 days. When I called to find out the status I was told the following:
She is in a meeting
She is working on it
Lots of calls to make for approval
She will call by end of day...that was Monday December 6th.
No calls, no resolution.  I have filed a complaint with the Better Business Bureau and asked for resolution.  
Don't let them scam you. They treat you like gold until you walk out the door. The service is a lot to be desired as well....It's all bad from start to finish. Verizon I will be back!!

I purchase (2) two galaxy S21 Ultra from T-Mobile and after two months we are experiencing echo when talking to each other or sometimes other friends and families. i called T-Mobile and express my concern, to make a long story short they change both phones, sim cards, walk me through change a bunch of settings in the phones, reset to factory settings, disable Bixby, network reset, sim reset and various other changes. now about 6 months later the echo is the same, hearing ourselves talking is the same, now T-MOBILE is tell me I should order a new phone and start over because there is nothing they can do for me. I've been with T-Mobile for over 20 years. they are tell me that my phone is out of warranty but refuse to accept thats the devices are defective. we this has been very inconvenient for us for months and they refuse to fix the problem. (**** T-MOBILE) I will find another service provider. i will never recommend t-mobile to anyone else

Look at the chat below. they make such promises 3 times and I am still struggling for my money. still waiting for a refund.

Monica C : Okay, we will make sure that it gets credited back to your card ending 2847 within the next 24 hours
Monica C : The request has been successfully forwarded to the concerned team, rest assured!
Monica C : The confirmation number is 6153498844
You : you are giving me the wrong confirmation, my card ending with 2487
Monica C : I am really sorry for the typo, yes, it is 2487
You : may be tomorrow you say Oh I credited to wrong account you didn't confirm the account
Monica C : Parul, I really apologize for that!
You : your whole chat is recorded. If I didn't get my refund in 24 hours then you would be responsible whatever I do to you.
Monica C : Please do not worry at all, your money will be refunded for sure!
Monica C : If you have any further question/doubts, let me connect you to our finance team for further assistance

This is the worst service I have ever seen.

They charged my account after I discontinued my services with them. When I asked them they said it will take 60 days to return my money. 

When I threaten them they said it will take 48-72 hours but it's 2 months now I am still struggling.

text: Worst phone provider I've ever used. Deceitful scammers who rope you into extra charges despite telling you your bill will be the same. Really wish they never bought out sprint.
T Mobile is the worst company. Money hungry, inconsiderate and worst service ever. As for their corporate office that deals with claims pathetic they don't answer calls at all

I had recently decided to switch to TMobile home internet. The equipment was shipped to my house. But before opening it I changed my mind an sent it back before starting the service. ( I had found a cheaper service). They cancelled it but never took my card off file for automatic payments. So after even though I never used it I got charged $50.00. I called an they said that they would then remove my card an issue a refund which would take 1-3 business days. On the fourth day I called again the next rep told me it would 7-10 business days??? Like Each rep had different info. WORST SERVICE I EVER HAD. P.S still waiting on the refund for service I never used! #T-moble

I recently visited the T-Mobile store located on Flagler and 100th Ave.  Miami.   I must say that I’m extremely satisfied with the attention and help received particularly from the Manager, but also the rest of the staff.  I hesitated before going given the reviews some people write, but in my case, and from comments made by regular customers there, I’m extremely happy I did business with them.

Wish I could have these two months of my life back since switching to T Mobile from Verizon. Was promised excellent coverage from home and work addresses from store mgr. Purchased 4  iPhone 12’s during their promotion. Subsequently, find out I cannot call or text. No bars from one address. 1 bar from the other. 7 weeks of repeated service calls and now stuck with phone bills and absolutely NO service. AVOID AT ALL COSTS.

Been with them for 23 years. In all that time they have been great, horrible, problem solvers and lately, they are a mess! Im not gong to rehash my latest issues that have been finally resolved. This complaint is about the app. Two things. First, No way to email them. The one issue that really kills me is if I call customer service and there is a wait time, 75% of the time I will be offered a call back option. But try to find that on the web site or the app. I just spent from 6:38pm until 7:22pm trying to find where that feature is? I have been told many times to go to the app and schedule that call back. Yeah OK where? I am so angry right now. The only good thing is I don't have to be asked by the customer service representative if I would tell my friends and family how great they are and recommend them? It is better not to say what my response has been to that statement.
Gerardo is the best sale associate super funny sweet kind and helpful highly recommend him

We have always had insurance for these phones. 2 weeks ago, one phone ( I have 2), the battery wouldn't last. It said 100% and then 0 % in less than a minute. So we qualified for a exchange warranty. What they gave us in exchange, was  Samsung A20 which I dont think was financially acceptable. They even give this phone free when you open an account. After speaking to a supervisor, the offered me a 500 dollar phone but I had to pay half of tgat amt.! I'm retired, I dont have that kind of money, thats why I pay insurance on the phones. I had no other choice but to stay with the A20. Very unacceptable.
Worst customer service I've ever experienced. Once my contract is done I am leaving.
The worst Cellular network ever calls drop all the time and their 5g coverage is a scam,AT&T is the best is my opinion

good evening this antonio fields, the reason for this is that is is the worst company ever in the world like i would not want people to get there phone from T- moblie at all yalll find a better company frfr like t-moblie is not a good company

T mobile deserves 0 stars...one of the worst companies I have ever dealt with.  They disconnected my phone by accident and I could not get it connected again.  I had to wait on hold 5 hours.  They are trash...never go to them, there are so many phone companies out there.  Do not go to this one.  They are absolute garbage.

I have been a Sprint customer since 1999. When Tmobile and Sprint merged I really didn't care. The last phone I bought from a Sprint store before the disappeared for good was a Samsung Galaxy S10+. I never had any trouble until I changed the SIMS card from Sprint to Tmobile which they were pushing all of their customers to do. I started getting weak signal strength dropped calls mobile internet outages in certain areas where I never had trouble before. My phone even crash and locked up for no reason except for no mobile network. Tmobile cell service is poor and very expensive for no better than it is. I changed to AT&T and never will go back to Tmobile.

At The Falls mall T-Mobil store Miami,Florida 
Agent Estrella Conminas spent 1 hour on the phone with billing department helping me on a issue that was going on for a month. She had the professionalism, courtesy, knowledge and customer service attitude and took care the problem from roots. She was actually loosing money with me because at the end found out she works on commission only and lost other clients coming to purchace. She is the best of the best. would make a great manager + higher. Thank you T-Mobil  
Estevez

T-Mobile put me in collections the moment I closed my account. I have a cell booster from 6 years ago. They never told me to return the device oh, they simply put me in collections. I called to straighten it out and they told me they sent me a return label which I never received. I asked them to send it again and they said they could not. They told me to return it to any store. The stores won't accept it because it's too old. It's an LTE booster. They still want cash from collections but won't accept their equipment back instead. Their service was terrible and very spotty and I finally switched to Verizon because I couldn't take the chance of being without service if my motorcycle broke down. This company has been terrible to deal with for years but the coverage seemed quick when I had it. But it never worked far from the freeway and it never worked in doors. Now that they've merged with Sprint which I've had as well it was time to go. They treated me terribly since. The kid at the store told me they couldn't accept it and I told him customer service said to return it to any store. So he offered to call his manager oh, and I said no will call T-Mobile customer service. It was 7:30 p.m. and since I wanted to call T-Mobile customer service instead of his manager he threatened to call the police and have me removed if I didn't leave the store at 8 p.m. when they closed. The only thing I did was say I wanted to call customer service from T-Mobile instead of listen to his manager on the phone. T-Mobile customer service overrides anything the local manager wants to say. Great local customer service as well if that's what you're looking for to have the cops called on you for no reason. Now the cops never actually did get called because I didn't do anything. But threatening your customers oh, awesome!

After going into the T-mobile store I was told what my bill would be what the promos are. Paid extra to help pay off the new IPhone12 difference, the basic version was 829.99 and we upgraded to the pro for an additional 170.00. We made an extra payment for the upgrade and taxes, 170 for the phone upgrade and 75 for taxes. Our bills kept coming and we were being charged for the phone which was supposed to be free. After a couple hours on the phone I was told that you cannot pay off the difference and the 170.00 dollars was sitting in t-mobiles account and they would use it at the end of the 24 months to make a payment. The corporate office and the physical locations can spin whatever story they want and then blame each other for the fault. All the time the customer is getting screwed.

I would beware of the upgrade I was told by Jason and Matt at the Fairfield store that if I did an upgrade with the IPhone 13 from the IPhone XR I would receive an $800 credit and an additional $136 when I sent my phone back in…this was on 9/25/2021. Well I received the $136 credit and when I went in the T-Mobile app it showed I still owed $900 for the phone I called Customer Service and was told I should have called in before going into the store. I went to the store yesterday and was told a manager would call me, I waited until 12 the next day and went into the store and spoke with Dennis Cropp, the store manager who wouldn’t give me the other employees last name only to be told they gave me the wrong information and he couldn’t stand by what they told me even though Matt was there and know what I was told!!! Is this how T-Mobile does business by lying to get you to upgrade and make sales? This is a gimmick now I’m being told they can switch me to a higher plan in order to get what I was told I qualified for from the beginning!!! Customers beware of their fraudulent practices!!! I will file a complaint with the Better Business Bureau also. This is horrible customer service and fraud!!!!

Was In the store in Hackettstown Newjersey and on October 12 around 1pm the young girl towards the back helped me and was not helpful at all and very rude and wasn't prossesnal at all will never go back to that store again

They knowingly denied having conducted a transaction to "Jump" from one lease to another, and refused to take in the old device after charging me for the new device. Neither the in-store representative nor the call-in representatives took ownership of issues. A very disappointing end to 19 of patronage.

Someone take my phone 2 pm and I try to call t mobile company to temp. Suspend my account before someone misuse my phone and I use phone some other people coustmer service recording say wait time 1 hour and 30minutes shame on you and your company and someone misuse my phone to much zelle money use cash app.

All was great for a while,  the last 8 months,  WORST CONNECTIVITY EVER.  I've had to enable WiFi calling in a hotel in a very populated area to make a phone call!  In a more rural area, I couldn't get service at all.  DO NOT RELY ON THIS COMPANY.  You're not safe with them   And you can't reach customer service unless you say the f-word in your texts and then they'll respond to scold you for language and still won't assist you!  Useless as tits on a bull,  avoid at all costs!

We're Sprint customers with paid for phones.  We received a letter explaining our phones wouldn't work after Dec. 31st.  We obtained crappy replacements, probably made in N Korea.  They drop calls and don't connect to internet.   After 15 hours in T-Mobile stores and untold hours on their 666 help line, we've given up.  They are a useless company.  We're going with a Competitor.

Very bad experience with the bill. They had changed our bill 4- 5 times and it is very frustrated to deal with T mobile. Sometimes the cell signal of function freezes . It was better when we had sprint . I will not recommend changing to T mobile since it seems they just like scammers, they only want you money and sign with them. I know they always say sorry they will fix this but it has been already 2 months and nothing has been done to change to my right bill. Whoever we talk to they always will say the same bs.

Having been a T-mobile, Verizon & AT&T customer. I’ve had the benefit of comparing all of the major US carriers and here are some of my thoughts & experiences as a T-mobile customer for the past year.

Pros:
- A pretty good value depending on your market.
- Their customer service team seems to try a little bit harder than most of the major carriers.
- T-mobile Tuesdays is a pretty sweet perk.

Cons:
- Data throttling will leave you extra frustrated in times of need.
- Service can be patchy in areas even if you live in a large urban metropolitan.
- Doesn’t value users data hence the bad rap with many data leaks.

I scored a great discount with my employer which ultimately was the main motivation to switchover to T-mobile. Perhaps the deal was too good as I got overzealous and added on my in-laws to my plan too. That changed my rate plan. After more than a total of 60+ hours working with customer service for a couple months, many unfulfilled escalation tickets, and manual bill credits. We were able to revert my plan back to the correct discount. The straw that broke the camel’s back was the data leak that left millions of customers personal information exposed and for me, that was not worth the headache. Not too mention the data throttling in times of need that would make loading just the Google landing page impossible. As I cut my relations with T-mobile after getting back my plan to $62/month for 5 lines (I know, it was a really good deal but not worth the headache to deal with T-mobile’s shenanigans) My final bill came out double what I usually pay although I cut my service 2 days after the service period. After calling the so called T-mobile “Experts” they were able to see my past billing payments and price but weren’t willing to make one final bill adjustment to reflect that previous monthly rate. That left a sour taste in my mouth about their business practices and I can confidently say, I will never consider T-mobile’s services for my wireless needs nor will I ever recommend their service to my family, friends and even enemies.

T-mobile is a bad company if you ever try to port your phone number away.

Your phone number is DIRECTLY linked to your account so when it's ported away, you can't pay your bill.

T-mobile has some of the worst service I've seen from a mobile phone company. I jumped ship and I'm happier with my cell service. 

I hope my 100$ bill from them costs them much, more more over time. I will always advise people to stay away because they are a poorly run company that doesn't actually care. There was ZERO attempt at retention.

Customer over 20 years and the new offer for iPhone that is supposed to also apply to old customers is once again a joke. Called customer retention and nothing. All they want is new money. False advertising. Even the employee  at T-Mobile agreed.

Is the T-Mobile customer service department actually in the witness protection program?  I tried to call in for support, but I passed away, from old age, before somebody came on the line to speak with me._
No stars was not an option. The 5G connection is more like 1G  (bar) in Danville Ca If they told you the phone was free with trade-in on the 55+ plan it’s a lie.

Sprint buyout and for awhile I was contacted about my phone sim card upgrade. I went in and right away they started upselling me and that was all they were interested in. I watched other people coming in and only get confused with what they were being told. I decided to look for a different carrier after over a decade with Sprint. Was it worth it T-Mobile ??

Amazon treats their customers with dignity and respect and they are actually interested in what their customers want. T Mobile used to be like that. Now they treat their customers with condescension and they tell them what they think they need instead of respecting what they actually want. While they make might make some short term money ultimately customers are going to leave. Amazon is a billion dollar company for their customer service. T Mobile has lost their respect for their customers. They are treating their customers like they were children and telling them what they're going to give them while they take their money and refuse to respect their wishes. Unbelievable

We were sprint customers and since TMobile bought them out, one of our phones they said would not work after January.  We had two options. They could switch out sun card or upgrade to new phone. Well we wanted to just switch out SIM card cause phone works fine and not that old.  We’ll they said no don’t have any SIM cards but for 130 you could get new phone.  It was a 600 promotion deal they had.  Well since we had no option but to get new phone we did. But then we had to get a new 20 plug that doesn’t come with the phone (scam) and then a new case and screen saver.  So total was over 300.  Then they kept our old phone that we paid for.  After it was done they told us that.  So we had to give up our old phone and pay 300 for them having taken over sprint.  Some customer service!! Costing the customer money for your switch over.

When i signed up on the 55 plan i was told no charge for the phone and no contract.I signed up at the beginning of the pandemic lock down. And had to do everything over the phone.The cell service was not as promised for my area and i payed for the extra screen protector up front but was billed every month for them and had to call their customer service.this happened every month.i had 2 phones for me and my wife.which were Motorola and they were fine. After over a year of putting up with there marginal service and billing mistakes i switched to another carrier and paid the final bill including the extras which i did not owe but didn't want to wait 45min to dispute them again.This was in may ,The only correspondence i received was emails asking me to come back.In October of 2021 i received a bill from a collector that i owed $234.00.Which was for the phone's.Tried callings T-mobile and like always the wait time was 1hr.I just paid the collector which i might add was very understanding and said that unfortunately he has heard my story concerning T-mobile alot we are seniors and in this age of technology companies take advantage of our lack of knowledge of .

Its actually NO STARS ...I went to them to get a new account only for me to change my mind ,they took my money for a phone they dont have then lied and said that its in the warehouse ONLY for me to cancel and now they talking bout they cant cancel cause the phone is still in the warehouse ...not shipped yet! THEY SUCK ..

The service is fine, customer service is fine too. What pisses me off is they say when you order a new phone it will he 2 days maybe 3 days until you get it. I ordered it Monday and it literally just shipped today so wi won't be getting it until this Monday.. thats my only issue, if you state something your going to do, I expect you to do what you said...even more so with a company such as T-Mobile.

Sold me a phone sim and a prepaid service knowing it t didn't work where I lived and then when I called they said no problem we can refund your money and waited and waited and even had a confirmation code but nothing so then when I called back with their fake code they said you are no longer a customer so we can't refund your account,wait what? Then they said at the BBB side they can't even find my confirmation code in the system when's I provided the actual receipt I had to beg them for and they said all I had was a sim card and said I was refunded for it and I was not? I would use every word in the dictionary to describe this company as horrible. This company is the dumbest most moronic stealing conniving and lying company I've ever had the displeasure of being stolen from. They are worse than someone mugging you on the street. I will never look at their sign or store front with wanting to vomit. Stop paying Tom Brady money and fix your company!!!! He obviously doesn't need the money!!!! Stay away read the reviews they are no worse than a person stealing out of your purse.

I was with sprint ,and my contract said that when I am done paying for my phones ,that they were my ,now that t-moble took over ,when I called them to ask why I am still paying for my phones ,they told me that I will continue making payments even when I paid them all the payments, and if I want to keep the phones I have to pay over $1,000 so I have to continue paying no matter what, 2 months ago I was told by a rep.that I will see my payments go down because I am done paying now t-moble is saying the opposite. And since t-moble took over ,my service is vary bad.they will not do anything about it and no credit. I will now leave t-moble and hire am attorney to go after them .I don't care how big they think they are.

Don't be full with their free phones ... even with free phone is too expensive...
It's the worst company with horrible service and internet so inconvenience and nerve wrecking...

edits.text:
This company is absolutely a disaster! Extremely slow internet,  dropped calls,  messages not delivered,  features don't work....
I would not recommend...

I have been with this company for 2 years, and it was ok! 
Recently after renewing my contract, it has gone down to the hill!  Sometimes I have to  wait for a long time to move ! 
It's awful i can  not wait another year of this! I am thinking of switching to another company! 
Right now, I  would give this company minus zero...

text: Had Sprint for 20 yes very happy with them.  Since t mobile took over it has been a nightmare.  Failed text but you don't know for minutes.  Can't use phone in my car for at least 5 minutes. Says I have no carrier.  Half the time Can't get sone internet sites.  T9 top it off I have always  bought my phones and paid monthly.  When contract time is up has been mine.  Now says I can upgrade or continue to pay lease.  I have never leased.  I've heard they were a good company but think they bit off more than they can handle with adding Sprint.  No customer service.  Can't get ahold of anyone.

Worst sales department ( telecelle ) customer service.  They treat you like you are so dumb and lies and gives fake promises just to make you sign up . The unprofessional sales department ( i believe operating from Philippines  ) will make all customers run away from t mobil as they are doing to me . Been a sprint loyal customer for over 12 years and never experienced anything like this craziness that im forced to deal with after t mobil merger.

Had Sprint for about 24 years.  Was happy.  This T-Mobile take-over has been beyond mismanaged, one unfulfilled promise after another and horrendous customer and cell service.  Since they switched from the Sprint to T-Mobile towers, I never again had more than two bars, often lose calls, I can hear people, the do not hear me, internet on phone is unresponsive, etc. etc. etc.  NUMEROUS calls to "service" and support, several trips to the store, they finally told me that despite living in a fairly metropolitan area and in a major, very large college town, T-Mobile has very few towers.  From store employee "well it's expensive to put all those new towers up so it's going to take some years to get good coverage in this area".  I'm done.  Going to Verizon this weekend.  Do not even consider T-Mobile - just drive on by!

Maddie in the Customer Service tried to load an Alcatel Joy Tab 2 worth $168 dollars with $7 per month installation on me telling me that it's free. Are you kidding me? This is not free, you are offloading something to me with misleading statement that it is free when it actually is not. How is this legal?

I have 11 lines that I wanted to move from Verizon.  I was given a personal contact to handle the transition as I was extremely skeptical that the move would go  smoothly.   Chelsea was extremely nice and very knowledgable.  She assured me that she would walk me thru the entire process.   Fast forward to when I got my first bill.   Surprise! not as quoted.   Surprise! Chelsea has handed me off to her Business support Team.   SURPRISE!!!  it is in the Phillipines and I can't talk to the same person twice.   After spending hours on the phone, I decided to cut my loss and cancel.  Now, I can't get thru to anyone at all.  No one will return my emails....This is ridiculous.  Run the other way if T-Mobile tries to reel you in with better pricing.

T-Mobile is the absolute worst cell phone company, I spoke with a representative via live chat, I was looking to upgrade all 3 lines in my T-Mobile account, The rep said sure absolutely no problem, I have upgraded all the lines and we will bill the devices to your account, a week later I did a follow up because I never received confirmation of my order, I then called to check the status of my order and the supervisor I spoke with told me, She’s not sure why the previous rep gave me that info, They cannot honor it, and I am not allowed to upgrade and be billed. Customer service means a lot to me, for a issue like this, is very misleading, I would recommend Verizon over T-Mobile any day.

My phone mysteriously lost service a month ago. I spent several hours on the phone with T-Mobile (and was transferred half a dozen times) to find out they never told me I needed a new SIM card. They did credit the account $10, as they admitted this was their fault. 

The rep I spoke to said I had to go into a T-Mobile store to get a new SIM card. I asked for it to be mailed. It's worth mentioning this option was not offered - I had to know to ask. 

When the new SIM card arrived, they hadn't activated it. I tried to activate it, but they couldn't send me an activation code because my phone had no service. This should be an obvious, reoccurring error that T-mobile can fix.

I went into the store today to have them activate the SIM card and I had to wait nearly half an hour to speak with anyone. Finally, when I tried to explain how the SIM activation process is flawed, the rep at the store ignored me. I told him "I haven't had service for a month because this process is not well thought-out" and his reply was "oh, were you on vacation?" The rep made no apology or effort to make things right - he attempted to change the subject and ignore my concerns. I asked if the process is purposely flawed to make people have to come into the store and he ignored me again.

I conclude that T-Mobile makes purposeful efforts to periodically disrupt service in order to force customers into the store and buy merchandise.

Tmobile was a great company the past year has been terrible.  Long wait times, unable to upgrade when upgrades are due. What happened to the so called dedicated team of experts? Were are the promised called backs to resolve issues? I am not happy with this company.

For me, customer service is everything. Most of the goods and services we use ate commodity, and work and even cost pretty much the same. So the real mark of qualities in a company is how they deal with issues as they arise. All things being equal, I haven’t really experienced customer service as responsive as T-Mobile’s. That doesn’t mean I always get what I want, but at least I know that someone with authority to do things has heard me. 

We have been customers for over nine years, and use them for both business and personal communications. There might be other companies or slightly better in some areas, but the combination of service and customer service means that I am happy

Most untrustworthy company I've ever dealt with. They promise you everything when you sign up but then they dont follow through with there word. They were supposed to pay our old phones off and contract with verizon and we havent recieved a dime. Cost me 1200.00 extra out of pocket because of that lie. 
     I wish I would've just stayed with verizon. Please avoid tmobile at all costs. They should not be getting more business when they lie and cheat to potential new customers.

Few months ago, due to the fact that I had to travel, I bought a 4G wireless internet access point device  from t-mobile. I had to drive to a local t-mobile store in order to purchase and enable the device, and while at the store I was asked to create a pass code (basically a 4 or 6 digit number). 

Upon my return from my trip, I called t-mobile in order to deactivate the device given that I did not need to use anymore for the time being, and while on the phone I was asked for the pass code (they could have used several other personal information but they only would accept the pass code) and once I provided the pass code the agent stated that it was incorrect. The agent then indicated that he/she could sent me another pass code to a device but since mine was an access point device and not a mobile phone that this option would be possible, and that in order to deactivate the device I would have to go to a local t-mobile store. Given the big inconvenient and opportunity cost that would represent for me having to stop everything I was doing in order to have to drive to a t-mobile store and once arriving there having to deal with the situation, etc, it took few days to be able to go to the store and resolve that issue.

It has been some time, so I do not really remember clearly what took place at the store; but as far as I can remember I explained the situation to the store employees and they asked for my id and made me call t-mobile from the in order to talk to a t-mobile agent over the phone to resolve the problem. I explained to the store employees and the agent over the phone that I would not pay for the excess days (and I did not use the device during that period) that was incurred since my first attempt to deactivate my device over the phone at home. As far as I can remember the agent over the phone was unwilling to extricate me from that unfair situation, but I made clear that I would pay for any excess charges. Anyhow, I asked the agent over the phone to deactivate the device; the agent then offered me the option of permanently deactivating the device which would then require me to buy a new sim card in case I would reactivate it in the future or temporary deactivate the device for a few months which would then allow me to use the same sim card once I decided to reactivate the device, I then choose the latter option. 

As aforementioned above, the excess time that was incurred since my first phone call and my second trip to the store simply was not resolved, and I have no intention whatsoever to pay for it for obvious reasons. Few weeks after my second visit to the store, I received  another bill from t-mobile by regular mail showing another bill for yet another month thus indicating that my device simply was not deactivated – which was the primary reason for my second visit to the store. I then proceeded to call t-mobile yet again from home, then they asked yet again for my pass code which I provided (I created a new one on occasion of my second visit to the store), and they told me yet again my newly created pass code was wrong therefore they could not help over the phone and I would have to go a local t-mobile store in order to sort out the problem. 

Suffice to say that I did not go to the store and simply ignored the problem altogether given that I believe that I took all reasonable and necessary steps to resolve that problem. Wasting my time  over the phone with t-mobile in one or two calls was as far as I would go to take care of this issue from that point on. Since I am not t-mobile’s driver I really do not see myself going back to their local store yet again. 

As of now, t-mobile is sending me collector letters (by a debt collector) in order to collect a debt that I really do not owe.  To  be quite frank and blunt, it really seems that t-mobile is making extremely difficult for people to deactivate or cancel accounts on purpose in order to overcharge them for services that they simply did not use. Ultimately, t-mobile is really practicing unscrupulous and unfair schemes in order steal people’s money.

So now T-Mobile is practicing in the art of "identity-theft" and fraud! I received an email with an attachment that was not signed by me, stating that I have entered into a "lease agreement" for my new phone. This agreement is dated 15 days after I switched carriers, complete with a $9 a month payment starting on Nov. 3rd. I was not in a TMobile store on 10/3, and this so-called lease is completely fake, fraudulent, and is illegal  I will be taking these lying thieves, and TMobile, to court. I am angry, no longer trust this company. Nothing but lies from T-Mobile and it's employees.

Also, this fraudulent document was created on the last day of their 15 day return policy, and then released to me after business hours two days later. All I know is, no jury will never exonerate TMobile for heinous act!!!

This was done in the store I went to and bought my 2 new devices and entered into a two year. Plan. The very store where I was lied to about the services I would be getting. That store is located on A1a in Cocoa Beach, Florida just north of 520 and Ron Jon's, at 5645 N. Atlantic Ave.

edits.text:
I am a new customer of T-Mobile. My experience is unacceptable! First off, the person in the store told me if I went on this 90 dollar magenta plan, I would get free Netflix for the life of the service, and it included 4 screens at the same time! Lie. I get one screen only, but hey, I bought 2 devices. Wow.
After complaining for 2 days, and their "experts" expertly misleading me with more lies, I am fed up. One even said she would call me back in one hour and have everything fixed. No call, just more deception.
Then there is the store where all of this started. They want me to come back and pay another 14 dollars in "sales tax" they forgot to collect for my new FREE phone. Ha!
So, I will be returning to the store, and return the phone and tablet, and covers, EVERYTHING I bought, cancelling this over priced service, get a full refund, and never look back as I run to my old carrier. I will pay less, and get treated with respect. T-Mobile was a mistake.
